123738.explanation: Residents must be given ample opportunities to participate and contribute their views on important issues through good communication and involvement. When this runs smoothly, a referendum becomes unnecessary and avoids unnecessary costs for organising local politics in a small municipality.

123744.explanation: Our municipality is too small for a permanent asylum seekers' centre (AZC), which can have a significant impact on residents and facilities, as we see in Maarheeze and Budel. Temporary shelter is possible, provided residents are informed and involved in advance.

123751.explanation: Festivals are a tradition in Someren and their number is limited. We want to cherish this without extra regulations, but safety and limiting nuisance remain important. Proper placement of sound sources and monitoring compliance with established noise standards help achieve this.

123753.explanation: We are positive about building houses on agricultural land as is already possible, but not every location is suitable or desirable. Provided it occurs under clear rules, with sensitivity to the environment, sufficient support, and reasonable costs, this proposal is conceivable.

123754.explanation: Sports are important for health and well-being, also for people receiving social assistance benefits. However, arrangements already exist to enable participation, so the municipality does not need to pay for additional memberships. Support remains important though.

123755.explanation: The local broadcaster is valuable and remains important for Someren, but additional financial support is not necessary. Thanks to modern media and connection to the regional broadcaster, under which SIRIS also falls, local news provision has become more diverse and accessible to residents.

123757.explanation: National arrangements already exist for children from lower-income families. For other children, music lessons remain a personal responsibility. The municipality can stimulate participation with trial lessons so that as many children as possible can be introduced to music and associations can also benefit.

123760.explanation: We preferably focus on having sufficient police presence, with greater visibility on the streets, in neighborhoods and rural areas, and good cooperation with existing enforcement officers (BOAs) to ensure effective supervision and safety, protecting residents.

123761.explanation: Privacy remains important and must be protected. At the same time, targeted camera surveillance at public high-risk locations can help improve safety and support enforcement. This way, residents are protected and crime can be tackled better.

123766.explanation: Although it seems like an obvious solution, permanent residence in holiday homes causes much uncertainty and problems in practice. We should focus on building new homes and avoid creating new housing issues for the future.

123767.explanation: We face a huge housing shortage. Infill within village boundaries is preferred, but new construction outside these borders is unavoidable. This provides sufficient housing and helps maintain liveability in small communities. However, it is important that this is done carefully.

123768.explanation: It is the municipality’s responsibility to help people back to work. However, if this fails, they can still be of great value. As volunteers, they have activities to engage in and at the same time ensure that the strength of society is optimally utilized.

123769.explanation: Where this already happens in Someren, it is a tailored solution for some residents who have difficulties. It represents a degree of freedom that implementers should have in the fight against poverty to achieve customized solutions. However, we do not allow it to become a permanent instrument.

123770.explanation: Prices keep rising, making help insurmountable for some people. As a municipality, we are responsible for offering assistance to those in need. However, the income threshold must not conflict with other benefits that residents may already be entitled to.

123772.explanation: Designating parking spots will cause the existing parking pressure in streets and neighborhoods to increase further. Instead, the municipality should establish clear policies to install charging facilities at strategic locations without taking away existing parking spots.

123775.explanation: By focusing on price rather than quality, youth care might not lead to improvement. This could increase costs later by requiring referral to more intensive youth care or more help to solve problems.

123776.explanation: In the municipality of Someren, the property tax (OZB) is not unnecessarily high. By making smart choices and selecting appropriate cuts, we have been able to prevent a large increase in property tax in recent years. That will also be the goal in the coming years, avoiding levying more property tax than necessary.

123777.explanation: The dog tax is intended for creating and maintaining facilities for dog owners. These facilities, such as dog exercise areas and bins for dog waste bags, deserve to be reviewed and improved where necessary.

123780.explanation: Priority for status holders puts extra pressure on the social housing stock, which we want to preserve primarily for residents with ties to Someren. Flexible housing can be used for status holders so they can still be provided with housing at the start of their life here.

123782.explanation: There are already sufficient solar panels on agricultural land in Someren. Not all are well integrated into the landscape. For this reason, new solar parks do not need to be allowed in the coming years, so we can preserve landscape and quality.

123786.explanation: The local dialect is part of our municipality's culture. It is good that attention is paid to it in schools.

123787.explanation: Since national regulations regarding coffee shops and cannabis policies are still unclear, establishment in Someren is not desirable. This could increase pressure on enforcement and possibly cause nuisance. Additionally, there is already provision in the region.

123789.explanation: Someren is a village municipality and all four villages must retain their village character. Building upwards is allowed but with limits. High residential towers do not fit this image.

123790.explanation: Room rental can contribute to better use of the existing housing stock, for example for young people and singles. At the same time, it can pressure liveability and parking. Therefore, it should only be allowed under clear conditions and in suitable locations.

123791.explanation: The principle is that there should be sufficient parking spaces. This means the center remains easily accessible, surrounding residential areas are not used as parking buffers, and greenery is not unnecessarily removed. Where necessary, a larger nearby parking lot can offer a solution.

123793.explanation: Currently, there is insufficient certainty about infrastructure and nature development. The canal dike and A67 are already fully congested; without the RBT and certainty that these will be addressed, the situation will only worsen. Economic and financial advantages do not outweigh this.

123794.explanation: The municipality previously allocated funds for a bus station and should not do so again before public transport and accessibility have improved. The goal must be a direct public transport connection through every village. Without this, access to the station for residents is limited.

123795.explanation: The pesticide ban falls under the national government. The municipality should not regulate this independently, as it could create large differences between municipalities causing inequality for farmers.

123796.explanation: A wind farm is conceivable if it becomes, among other things, a social wind farm where the community and municipality financially benefit and participation is properly arranged. However, the way it has proceeded in recent years offers little prospect of realization under these conditions.

123797.explanation: If an entrepreneur complies with laws and regulations, hospitality businesses can be allowed on former livestock farms. Like campsites or shops, this can give an extra boost to tourism, provided liveability and the environment are not affected.

123738.explanation: We involve citizens in important decisions through participation. Residents must be involved at the earliest possible stage in important or impactful decisions. A referendum usually leads to popular opinions and does not foster connection.

123744.explanation: We have been housing asylum seekers for several years and are therefore taking our responsibility. A participation process is ongoing in which residents are consulted on this topic. An asylum seekers' center is not considered appropriate.

123751.explanation: This prevents noise nuisance and also avoids hearing damage. Considering the number of cases of hearing impairment and tinnitus, stricter standards are advisable.

123753.explanation: The municipality must assess where housing development is desirable. Generally, we want as much as possible to be built within the built-up area so that the countryside remains countryside.

123754.explanation: This promotes social contacts, creates connection and enjoyment of life. Additionally, it increases the chance that people leave a benefit dependency situation.

123755.explanation: We already do this. Extra measures are not necessary; we must use our resources sustainably.

123757.explanation: Music education contributes to the development and formation of children. It exposes them to culture from which they will benefit throughout their lives.

123760.explanation: More enforcement officers (BOAs) work preventively against nuisance and crime on the one hand and provide a sense of safety for residents on the other.

123761.explanation: Privacy is important. There are already increasing numbers of cameras in public spaces through smart doorbells and the like.

123766.explanation: Holiday homes are meant for recreation, not permanent residence. Holiday parks were developed to stimulate the recreational sector and the local economy. Allowing permanent residence would undo this. Permitting it leads to nuisance and complaints.

123767.explanation: Housing construction should take place as much as possible within the village boundaries. Planned developments, such as Someren-Noord, must integrate well with the existing core.

123768.explanation: Socially meaningful work provides satisfaction and fosters connection. It also increases the chance of getting out of benefit dependency.

123769.explanation: We must not facilitate debt problems. It is better to support people facing debt issues.

123770.explanation: We have already done this for various schemes in recent periods.

123772.explanation: We are already developing policies for this. The municipality must facilitate the installation of more charging points. However, this should not lead to a general shortage of parking spaces.

123775.explanation: Not only the price but also the quality of care and the achieved results.

123776.explanation: As far as we are concerned, the property tax (OZB) is the balancing item of the budget. It should only increase if absolutely necessary.

123777.explanation: We also incur costs for dogs. Think of the construction of dog walking areas and emptying trash bins with dog waste. We place those costs where they belong: the dog owner.

123780.explanation: We must meet the regional obligations assigned to us. In recent years, this has been achieved in cooperation with Wocom. However, we want to prevent homelessness and ensure urgency.

123782.explanation: This is already prohibited based on national policy. Solar panels should be placed on roofs as much as possible.

123786.explanation: It is already quite a challenge to bring the language level to a good standard, let alone paying extra attention to dialect.

123787.explanation: A small municipality is not designed to handle the possible nuisance this brings. If national policy provides more clarity (legalizing rather than tolerating), it might be different.

123789.explanation: Closer to the center, it is possible to build higher up to 4 and perhaps even 5 stories. However, we want to preserve the village character and the identity of Someren.

123790.explanation: Let’s first focus more on the possibility to divide houses. Someren is not a student city.

123791.explanation: Someren is growing, tourism is increasing, and thus the need for parking spaces in the center is rising. Many people depend on cars and must be able to park them.

123793.explanation: There is great demand for industrial land. It offers opportunities for companies outgrowing their local sites to relocate and free up space for smaller businesses. We are part of the region and also reap the benefits (more than 2 million OZB/year).

123794.explanation: To a limited extent, we should contribute here. Public transport is also important for Someren, with many lines converging in Eindhoven.

123795.explanation: We follow national policy here. We don’t refer to pesticides but to plant protection products. Agriculture cannot yet do entirely without them. The municipality itself no longer uses such products for weed control on paved areas.

123796.explanation: Years ago, we set the conditions under which we are willing to cooperate on the Wind Park. These conditions still apply. If they are not met, we will not cooperate.

123797.explanation: We do not find it desirable for hospitality establishments to arise everywhere in the countryside. We provide space for tourist initiatives with possibly supporting hospitality. This contributes to the rural economy and can be enriching.

123738.explanation: Residents must definitely have the opportunity to express their opinions. Citizens elect council members to represent them and to make important decisions with all the knowledge they have.

123753.explanation: This must fall within municipal policy, otherwise there will be uncontrolled proliferation.

123754.explanation: We have our minimum income policy for that.

123766.explanation: You could (temporarily) try this as a pilot to meet the greatest need.

123767.explanation: With this housing shortage, that is very undesirable.

123768.explanation: Provided this is (mentally or physically) possible.

123772.explanation: This must grow along with the increase of electric cars. We advocate for a pilot not to place a sign by the charging station, so that non-electric cars cannot park there either.

123775.explanation: Quality is also very important in this.

123780.explanation: Status holders are simply residents of the Netherlands. Equal treatment with other residents of the Netherlands is therefore fair. So also 'just' on the waiting list like everyone else.

123782.explanation: First make maximal use of solar panels on roofs.

123789.explanation: It is unclear what is meant by high residential flats.

123791.explanation: Comply with the applicable parking standard for new developments.

123793.explanation: Only if it meets all the requirements we set is this conceivable. Among others, good traffic flow, preventing through traffic via Lierop, and ensuring green, water management, and cost neutrality must be arranged according to frameworks; otherwise no RBT.

123794.explanation: This is currently no longer relevant as it has already been decided. Contributions to the region are only conceivable if there is reciprocity. So if Someren receives financial contributions in return.

123795.explanation: Someren must follow national policy in this matter.

123796.explanation: It is conceivable if it meets all conditions and if research shows that it does not pose a health risk to residents living nearby.

123797.explanation: If this fits within the current policy, it should sometimes be possible with custom measures.

123738.explanation: Depending on the subject, this should be a possibility.

123744.explanation: We are not in favor of a permanent asylum seekers center. Someren already complies with the legal obligation under the distribution law by enabling temporary accommodation for short periods.

123751.explanation: The Someren standard of 102 dB aligns with national agreements but can already cause hearing damage with prolonged exposure. The Health Council therefore advises a lower standard of about 100 dB, and we believe this advice deserves to be followed.

123753.explanation: Housing is one of the options especially for retiring farmers to use their land, for example in the form of courtyards. However, this must not cause problems for permits of other farming businesses.

123754.explanation: We consider sports very important. Many arrangements already exist for this, such as the 'Regeling Meedoen' in Someren. Additionally, people on welfare benefits can also turn to specialized funds such as the Youth Fund and Sport & Culture, Stichting Leergeld, and Sam & Adult Sport & Culture Fund.

123755.explanation: The local broadcaster Siris is currently already financially supported. At the same time, more local media are emerging; it is important that these also get a chance. If there is substantiated reason for extra funding, we are open to that. Local news is very important!

123757.explanation: Music lessons are important and promote development. Many arrangements are already available for this, such as the 'Regeling Meedoen' in Someren. Additionally, people on welfare benefits can also access specialized funds such as the Youth Fund and Sport & Culture, Stichting Leergeld, and Sam &.

123760.explanation: More police, yes; more public order officers (boa's), no.

123761.explanation: We are in favor of extensive camera surveillance at least on all our industrial estates. Preferably aligned with how it is permanently organized in Helmond via the Helmond Industrial Estates Foundation.

123766.explanation: The minister will soon oblige municipalities by instruction rule to allow existing occupancy of recreational homes under conditions. So yes, it must be allowed but under strict conditions.

123767.explanation: Courtyards and homes (formerly space-for-space homes) must be permitted outside existing village boundaries. Besides, we will never be able to achieve the desired housing growth within the existing village boundaries.

123768.explanation: People on welfare benefits can give something back to society through socially meaningful work.

123769.explanation: Everyone pays taxes, including people with lower incomes. It should be assessed per situation, but not waived.

123770.explanation: Work must pay off. Raising it creates a problem for some groups in Someren: the problem of a poverty trap due to losing other income.

123772.explanation: Indeed, where housing makes it impossible. But it must not lead to more parking pressure. New spots must be discussed with the neighborhood in advance. Also, promote charging spots at people’s homes who own a home battery with a surplus.

123775.explanation: Price is an important criterion, but quality is just as important. LSH wants care with the best price-quality ratio.

123776.explanation: Someren’s current financial situation unfortunately does not allow this yet. But ultimately, the property tax (OZB) must be lowered or at least not rise above annual inflation.

123777.explanation: Within built-up areas, various facilities are made for dog owners and a fee may be charged for this.

123780.explanation: Housing is initially allocated to our residents or those with a strong social and/or economic bond. LSH uses the 2014 Housing Act to ensure that status holders do not receive priority in housing allocation.

123782.explanation: LSH is a strong advocate of solar panels on roofs. Someren already has enough solar parks on agricultural land; no more are needed.

123786.explanation: LSH is in favor of preserving our dialect.

123787.explanation: LSH has no moral or ethical objections to the establishment of a coffee shop within our municipal boundaries. Still, we do not consider allowing a coffee shop as an end in itself.

123789.explanation: We support building upwards at and around central locations in the villages (at a scale/height appropriate for Someren). Building upwards is also an option at new housing estates like Someren-North as a way to complete a neighborhood or as a landmark.

123790.explanation: Host renting or renting out rooms must be allowed. There is a Housing Regulation that enables a municipality to manage the distribution of houses. It can include rules about where and how many households may live in one house. Also, that local residents get priority.

123791.explanation: We focus on accessibility by car (including parking). Nearly all parking issues in our municipality are found in Someren’s center. Since public transport within Someren is not yet a competitive alternative to the car, car dependency in the municipality is high.

123793.explanation: The regional industrial estate in its current nature, size, and location should not be developed.

123794.explanation: Only when bus lines and services to Eindhoven Central Station are expanded from Someren is co-payment justifiable.

123795.explanation: The physical living environment must be central, with fewer and clearer rules and more space for local customization. Agricultural entrepreneurs invest in innovations to use fewer resources; extra municipal restrictions in the zoning plan are considered unlikely, let alone prohibitions.

123796.explanation: No wind turbines in the Diepenhoek. Wind turbines of this height and capacity must always be at a sufficient distance from homes and near users of the electricity instead of just feeding back into the grid. Neither is possible in the Diepenhoek.

123797.explanation: Allow if it fits within existing municipal guidelines for hospitality and recreation and if it does not impose restrictions on existing businesses and/or homes.

123738.explanation: We stand for a governance culture where the municipality is close to the residents. Therefore, we favor involving residents in what is going on. A referendum can contribute to this.

123744.explanation: The Distribution Act applies and probably will remain so. This means it is legally mandatory to accommodate refugees. The temporary shelter in De Hoof shows that refugee accommodation is possible without causing problems!

123751.explanation: The Public Health Service (GGD) advises the municipality on noise standards for events to prevent hearing damage. Compliance with these standards must be enforced.

123753.explanation: This is already possible when it fits within municipal policy and, if not, may possibly be arranged via an environmental permit. It always remains location-dependent.

123754.explanation: Everyone must be able to participate. So also be able to play sports. Sports are good for health and also serve a preventive purpose.

123755.explanation: It is very important that local media are supported in light of their oversight role over, for example, the municipality.

123757.explanation: Music lessons are good for children's development. Unfortunately, good music lessons are not affordable for every parent. Therefore, it would be beneficial to ensure through subsidies that every child can receive music lessons.

123760.explanation: Rules must be enforced. With the current number of enforcement officers (BOAs), this often proves difficult in practice.

123761.explanation: Camera surveillance should only be used when it is necessary for safety reasons.

123766.explanation: Basically, it is undesirable for holiday homes to be used as permanent residences. But in practice, this unfortunately does occasionally happen due to the housing crisis.

123767.explanation: Addressing the housing crisis is the top priority. Therefore, it is desirable to build outside the existing village boundaries as well. Plans for this are already in development, for example in Someren-North or Lierop-South.

123768.explanation: We have a positive view of people. Most people who end up on social assistance ultimately want to work again. Therefore, it is not fair to oblige them to do socially useful work.

123769.explanation: The strongest shoulders must bear the heaviest burdens. Abolishing the scheme would unnecessarily increase the financial pressure on residents with low incomes.

123770.explanation: Currently, the limit in Someren is 120%. Unfortunately, this limit often creates a poverty trap because when people earn a little more, they actually end up with less income in the end. By raising the limit to 130%, the transition becomes gradual and working becomes worthwhile.

123772.explanation: We support more parking spaces with charging points to accelerate the transition to electric driving. At the same time, we must consider residents for whom an electric car is not yet affordable.

123775.explanation: Although price must also be taken into account when making a selection, for us quality should take precedence.

123776.explanation: Lowering the property tax would mean cuts would have to be made elsewhere. We want to prevent (unnecessary) cuts, especially in the social domain.

123777.explanation: The dog tax is an outdated tax and unfair compared to residents with other (domestic) animals such as horses and/or cats, for which no tax is charged.

123780.explanation: The rules regarding priority for social rental housing should be the same for everyone. It is discriminatory to exclude status holders from this. Status holders have the right to priority just like other residents with an urgency declaration.

123782.explanation: We face a huge task to make our energy supply sustainable. Generating sustainable energy on agricultural land contributes to the energy transition.

123786.explanation: We think it is up to the schools themselves whether they want to pay attention to the Zummers dialect. The municipality is basically only responsible for the housing of schools.

123787.explanation: We are in favor of legalization. If an entrepreneur judges that there is demand for a coffeeshop, we believe this should be possible.

123789.explanation: One way to build more homes is to build higher within the core. This contributes to addressing the housing crisis. Naturally, we must consider the size and scale of Someren.

123790.explanation: If there is a need for shared housing, it should be possible. By formally allowing this, rules can be established and illegal subletting to, for example, migrant workers can be prevented.

123791.explanation: We support making the center car-restricted to create more space for pedestrians, cyclists, and greenery. Adding more parking spaces does not fit with this.

123793.explanation: We are critical about the possible arrival of the business park. We have concerns about the possible arrival of large-scale logistics, traffic effects, and impacts on the surrounding area.

123794.explanation: We look positively at the regional cooperation in the Brainport region. However, a contribution has already been made to the bus station in Eindhoven. As far as we are concerned, it stays that way. For a new mobility agenda, we will contribute if there is a balanced package.

123795.explanation: Unfortunately, it is not yet known whether many pesticides have no negative effects on the health of people, animals, and plants. Therefore, we only want to allow the use of low-risk or organic agents.

123796.explanation: It is necessary to generate more sustainable energy. This also requires generating wind energy on land. Someren has previously designated the Diepenhoek as an area where wind energy can be generated. Part of the agreements is that public wind turbines will also be installed.

123797.explanation: We support helping farmers transition to less intensive farming, but any approach always depends on the location.

123738.explanation: This can be done through an advisory referendum. We must be cautious with binding referenda, but we do not rule them out.

123744.explanation: Only limited temporary shelter. This may be possible in specific cases after consultation with residents.

123751.explanation: If these take place near residential areas and protected natural areas, maybe yes. But it must remain feasible for the organizers.

123753.explanation: Not unlimited and always in consultation with the municipality. Dividing former farms into multiple homes must be made as easy as possible.

123754.explanation: Yes. That is better than granting a standard amount regardless of whether someone participates in this kind of activity.

123755.explanation: Only if necessary. The local broadcaster can largely support itself through advertising income. If the municipality becomes too much of a financier, the broadcaster may become too dependent on the municipality and could potentially lose impartiality in news coverage.

123757.explanation: It belongs in the regular curriculum. We believe it is not directly a municipal task. Outside school, we think the municipality can indeed contribute, and this should apply to everyone.

123760.explanation: If useful and necessary: definitely. And issue fewer fines that are basically only for scoring or financial reasons. No more nonsense fines. Enforcement officers should be deployed more usefully. Then you probably won’t need (as many) of them.

123761.explanation: As little as possible. It infringes on privacy too much. Only where necessary.

123766.explanation: But only for personal use. Not for rental. And only if using such a home frees up another one.

123767.explanation: Depends on the situation. We do not want to put a strict lock on this.

123768.explanation: We expect effort as a counterpart from everyone who receives benefits. We make an exception for cases where this is truly impossible.

123769.explanation: If people really cannot pay, they should not be burdened with this kind of charges. However, good control against abuse must be in place. By not making people pay these taxes, we also keep them out of debt restructuring and other troubles.

123770.explanation: Let’s deliver customized solutions so that everyone is helped as well as possible without creating unnecessary and unworkable obstacles.

123772.explanation: There are not many charging points in Someren, so more are needed. Let’s not forget that current electric cars will no longer be produced in a few years, reducing the need for these charging stations then. So let's not waste money by investing too much in this.

123775.explanation: As long as the necessary quality is not compromised, yes. It is still community money and should be spent as well as possible.

123776.explanation: Only if financially feasible. The property tax (OZB) should never be the balancing item in a budget. It must be fair and affordable and above all remain so.

123777.explanation: As far as we are concerned, yes. We have taken a first step by distributing the rates more fairly. Abolition is the next step in our opinion.

123780.explanation: Certainly not. Just adjust and fall in line. That is part of integration. Just participate without privileges. Everyone has equal rights and obligations.

123782.explanation: As little as possible. Preferably solar panels on roofs and no good fertile agricultural land sacrificed for solar panels.

123786.explanation: Let's keep it to acquiring basic knowledge at school and do this as well as possible. Improvement is already necessary here. So there really is no time for dialect.

123787.explanation: Yes. That way you prevent shady street trading and the criminal circuit has less influence. You also combat nuisance this way.

123789.explanation: Are often undesirable and undermine the village character.

123790.explanation: Under clear and strict conditions. Landlord exploitation and tenant abuse must be prevented.

123791.explanation: Maybe in some places. The real necessity is not there yet. For less able people, we must ensure they can fully participate by adequately spreading parking spaces.

123793.explanation: Yes. Our local businesses that want to expand are given the opportunity here, freeing up smaller spaces for startups and companies that need less space. For the municipality: extra property tax income.

123794.explanation: This is of regional importance and Someren also benefits from it. How much Someren should contribute is another matter. It must be proportional.

123795.explanation: Certainly if they are harmful to health and the environment. Also if they are unnecessary and can be replaced by other control methods, we support a ban.

123796.explanation: Can we do otherwise? Unfortunately, it has been forced upon us.

123797.explanation: Yes, if it is not harmful to the environment (people, plants and animals) and neighbors’ living comfort. Operators must comply with the same standards as hospitality venues in the center.

statements.title: Someren should allow hospitality businesses on former livestock farms in the countryside.

titleParts.glossary: Farms where animals are kept, such as cows or pigs.
Area within the municipality's borders but outside the towns or villages themselves; there are few houses and other buildings here.

statements.title: Noise standards at festivals and parties must be made stricter.
The residents of Someren should be able to express their opinion on important issues through a referendum.

titleParts.text: De inwoners van Someren moeten over belangrijke onderwerpen hun mening kunnen geven via een

titleParts.glossary: A public consultation where residents can express their opinion on a political issue. The municipal council is not obliged to follow the result; legally it cannot.

statements.title: There should be more municipal enforcement officers (BOAs) in Someren who focus on safety.

titleParts.glossary: People who, for the municipality, check whether citizens and businesses comply with the rules.

titleParts.text: (boa’s) komen die zich met veiligheid bezighouden.

statements.title: An asylum seekers' center (AZC) should be established in our municipality.

titleParts.glossary: Reception center for asylum seekers. They live there until it is decided whether they may stay in the Netherlands. An asylum seekers’ center remains in a municipality for a long time.

statements.title: It should be possible for farmers to build houses themselves on their land.
There should be more camera surveillance in Someren.

titleParts.glossary: Monitoring of a building, area, or (groups of) people using security cameras.

statements.title: The municipality should pay the membership fees for a sports club for people receiving social assistance.

titleParts.text: De gemeente moet het lidmaatschap van een sportclub betalen voor mensen met een

titleParts.glossary: Money from the municipality for people who have no income or personal funds.

statements.title: Someren should contribute to the cost of a bus station in Eindhoven.
The municipality should prevent new construction outside existing village boundaries.
The municipality should support the local broadcaster with additional funding.

titleParts.glossary: Radio and television by and for people from the municipality and the immediate area.

statements.title: People receiving social assistance should, in return, do socially useful work.

titleParts.glossary: Money from the municipality for people who have no income or personal funds.
For example cleaning the streets or keeping company and supporting elderly residents.

statements.title: Someren should ban the use of pesticides.
The municipality must allow holiday homes to be used as a permanent residence.
Every primary school child should be able to receive music lessons subsidized by the municipality.

titleParts.glossary: Money the government gives to citizens, businesses, and organizations to support them.

titleParts.text: van de gemeente muziekles kunnen krijgen.

statements.title: The municipality should stop forgiving municipal taxes for low-income residents.

titleParts.text: De gemeente moet stoppen met het

titleParts.glossary: That people no longer have to pay for something.
Taxes you have to pay to the municipality (e.g., sewer charge, waste disposal charge, dog tax).

titleParts.text: voor inwoners met een laag inkomen.

statements.title: The municipality should make it possible for all primary schools to teach or include the Someren dialect.
The municipality should raise the income threshold for receiving poverty assistance.
The municipality should abolish the dog tax.

titleParts.glossary: Tax paid to the municipality for owning one or more dogs.

statements.title: The municipality should allow landlords to rent out individual rooms instead of entire houses.
The municipality should designate more parking spaces as charging points for electric cars.
The regional business park at Lungendonk should be built.
The municipality should give refugees with a residence permit priority for social housing.

titleParts.text: De gemeente moet vluchtelingen met een

titleParts.glossary: The right to reside in the Netherlands. The term can refer to the right itself or to the card that shows someone has that right.
A rental home with a low rent (in 2026 at most €932.93 per month) for people with a low income.

statements.title: High residential apartment buildings should be allowed in Someren.
When selecting organizations for youth care, the municipality should primarily pay attention to price.
The municipality must allow solar panels on agricultural land.
The property tax (onroerendezaakbelasting, OZB) should be reduced.

titleParts.glossary: Tax you pay as the owner of a building or piece of land, or as the user of a commercial property.

statements.title: The municipality should allow the establishment of a coffeeshop.

statements.title: More parking spaces should be created in the center.
